meta.title: Mercedes-Benz M282 Engine — Specs, Reliability & Replacement Cost

hero.h1: Mercedes-Benz M282 Engine — Specs, Reliability & Replacement Cost
hero.subHeadline: The Mercedes M282 is a 1.3-litre turbocharged four-cylinder — efficient and responsive, but thermostat housing and oil consumption are common concerns. Backed by real UK specialist data [ESTIMATED].

rows.value: Shared with Renault/Nissan (H5Ht / HR13DDT) — exclusive to Mercedes in this ecosystem
Confidence: 74% confidence — based on UK specialist and owner-forum reporting, cross-referenced with recall and TSB data.

rows.metric: Mixed: some repairs straightforward (thermostat, sensors); others more involved (timing chain, carbon cleaning)

rows.value: Well-supported via Mercedes dealer network; shared with Renault/Nissan increases aftermarket availability

rows.metric: Reconditioned cost as % of lowest host value — £3,900 on a £3,500–£4,000 car = 98–111% → 4

rows.value: One-line verdict: "The M282 is an efficient, responsive modern turbo-four — but thermostat housing failures and oil consumption are well-documented — buy with full service history and warranty."

rows.metric: Best For: Urban commuters and buyers wanting a modern, efficient Mercedes with low running costs.
rows.value: Avoid If: Buyers seeking long-term high-mileage durability without proactive maintenance or those who dislike turbo lag.

compatibility.crossBrandNote: Cross-link note: For Renault and Nissan versions of this engine, see separate resources outside this site.

costGuide.labourEstimate: Fitting an M282 typically takes 8–12 hours at £65–£95/hr (independent) or £110–£160/hr (London/SE dealer) [ESTIMATED].
costGuide.sharedCostNote: Parts are shared with Renault/Nissan HR13DDT variants, potentially improving aftermarket availability and keeping costs competitive.

cta.label: → Compare M282 engine prices from UK Mercedes specialists

items.question: How much does an M282 engine replacement cost?
items.answer: A reconditioned M282 typically costs £2,500–£5,000 supply only, with fitting adding £600–£1,100 depending on labour rates [ESTIMATED]. Used examples can be found from £1,000; rebuilds range from £3,500–£6,000.

items.question: Is the M282 a reliable engine?
items.answer: The M282 rates 3.0★ out of 5 — a modern, efficient turbo-four with good performance, but known for thermostat housing coolant leaks, oil consumption, and carbon buildup on intake valves due to direct injection. Regular maintenance is essential.

items.question: What is the most common M282 failure?
items.answer: Thermostat housing coolant leaks are the most common issue — plastic housing cracks cause coolant loss and potential overheating. Camshaft centre bolt faults (causing loss of power and engine light) and turbocharger oil feed line leaks (recalled on 2019–2020 models) are also well-documented.

items.question: Which Mercedes models use the M282?
items.answer: The M282 powers the A‑Class W177/V177 (A 180/A 200), B‑Class W247 (B 180/B 200), CLA C118/X118 (CLA 180/CLA 200), GLA H247 (GLA 180/GLA 200), and GLB X247 (GLB 180/GLB 200) — spanning 2018 to present.

items.question: How long does the M282 last?
items.answer: With proper maintenance, 150,000–200,000 miles is achievable. Carbon buildup on intake valves typically requires cleaning around 60,000–80,000 miles; thermostat housing should be inspected at similar intervals. Oil consumption often increases past 100,000 km (~62,000 miles).
